summary_text: **Findings (ordered by severity)** - High: `2026-04-01 22:00` is already occupied by an `approved` post in the live queue (id `3316b8c0f0ac8103b66fc3368869cb7d`). Adding another post and later approving it for the same slot creates a duplicate-approved-slot condition. The scheduler only alerts on this; it does not block. See duplicate-slot alert logic in [scheduler.py#L192](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L192) and call site [scheduler.py#L567](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L567). - High: Tie handling for due `approved` posts at the same scheduled minute is not explicitly deterministic by business rule; scheduler picks `min(...)` from candidates by datetime only, so equal timestamps depend on candidate order. See [scheduler.py#L224](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L224) and [scheduler.py#L268](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L268). - High (policy): Quote-post authoring rule is strict: URL must not be in body, must be in `source_url` / Notion `Source URLs`, and manual registration must be `draft`. See [CLAUDE.md#L108](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/CLAUDE.md#L108), [CLAUDE.md#L119](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/CLAUDE.md#L119), [CLAUDE.md#L130](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/CLAUDE.md#L130). - Medium: Runtime enforcement is softer than policy; scheduler auto-extracts quote URL from body into `source_url` before validation, so body-URL violations can “self-heal” operationally. That reduces immediate failure risk but is still policy-noncompliant authoring. See [scheduler.py#L356](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L356) and [scheduler.py#L591](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L591). - Medium: Single-writer rule prohibits running scheduler on MBP; only Mac mini scheduler is the posting path. Any manual operation here must stop at draft creation/update. See [CLAUDE.md#L5](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/CLAUDE.md#L5), [CLAUDE.md#L13](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/CLAUDE.md#L13). **Safest compliant path** 1. Do not create a second `approved` post at `22:00` on April 1, 2026. Resolve slot ownership first (replace or reschedule existing approved item). 2. Create the new quote-post as `draft` only, with: - `text`: no `x.com` URL in body - `source_url`: `https://x.com/jack/status/2039004606890463261?s=20` - `scheduled_for`: leave empty until slot conflict is resolved (or assign a non-conflicting time) 3. If `22:00` is mandatory, convert the current `22:00 approved` item to another slot/status first, then set this new draft to `22:00`, then approve intentionally. 4. Keep Notion as SoT (`push_new_to_notion` / page property updates), then local cache follows; this matches the repo contract. See [CLAUDE.md#L171](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/CLAUDE.md#L171) and [notion_sync.py#L626](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L626).
